metric_value: Remote node solutions dominate corporate remote access environments
notes: Aberdeen's baseline market characterization at study date

metric_value: Move to remote node sacrifices important benefits provided by remote control
notes: Based on Aberdeen end-user research with IT managers and users

metric_value: 28.8 kbps; most remote connections run at this speed
notes: Quantitative baseline for bandwidth constraint analysis

metric_value: Large data volumes stream continuously across slow link; clogs remote pipes for bandwidth-intensive apps
notes: Aberdeen's analysis of remote node traffic pattern weakness

metric_value: Almost continuous but small traffic flow; only GUIs mouse movements and keystrokes traverse link
notes: Key advantage of remote control over remote node for bandwidth efficiency

metric_value: Enables full advantage of corporate desktop horsepower from remote location; computing done at corporate site
notes: Advantage for users with underpowered home/remote office equipment

metric_value: Support personnel can configure fine-tune and update remote devices via remote control; take control to diagnose problems
notes: Aberdeen endorses LapLink's helpdesk remote control capability

metric_value: Considered secure because users subject to same LAN security; but captured data streams more harmful than screen/mouse captures
notes: Aberdeen notes remote node's data stream interception risk

metric_value: Remote control over remote node provides encryption access control and parameter-setting for heightened security
notes: LapLink's hybrid architecture security advantage

metric_value: 10% or higher concentration of mobile users needing LAN application access when out of office
notes: Criterion 1 of Aberdeen's five-factor decision framework for adopting remote control over remote node

metric_value: Access to large databases and legacy applications from remote locations is required
notes: Criterion 2 of Aberdeen's five-factor decision framework

metric_value: Software/systems upgraded every 90-120 days
notes: Criterion 3 of Aberdeen's five-factor decision framework; remote control simplifies update distribution

metric_name: Decision criterion 4: continuous remote support need
metric_value: IT needs to provide continuous support to remote users
notes: Criterion 4 of Aberdeen's five-factor decision framework

metric_value: Data interception by outsiders is a concern
notes: Criterion 5 of Aberdeen's five-factor decision framework

metric_value: Traveling Software's LapLink positioned as enabling remote control over remote node hybrid architecture
notes: Aberdeen's framing of LapLink as the hybrid solution; study is vendor-sponsored content for Traveling Software

metric_value: Remote control implementation on the rise as business users frustrated with 28.8kbps data streaming
notes: Aberdeen predicts increasing enterprise adoption of remote control solutions

metric_value: Remote Desktop Protocol (RDP) and Citrix ICA became dominant enterprise remote access; remote node/dial-up displaced by VPN over broadband by 2003-2005
notes: Aberdeen's prediction directionally correct (remote control dominance) but mechanism was VPN+broadband+RDP not dial-up remote control

metric_value: LapLink positions as solving core remote access cost/time/experience problems for IT
notes: Aberdeen implicitly endorses LapLink as enterprise remote control solution

metric_value: Traveling Software renamed Laplink Software circa 2003; active in 2026 as PC migration software vendor; partnerships with Microsoft (2014) and Intel (2022)
notes: Company survived but pivoted from remote access to PC migration (PCmover); still headquartered in Bellevue WA; the specific remote control over remote node use case was displaced

metric_value: Dial-up remote node (RAS) displaced by IPSec VPN over DSL/cable broadband by 2003; modem-era constraints that justified hybrid approach became irrelevant
notes: The entire problem space Aberdeen documented was solved by bandwidth expansion rather than architectural layering
